params.description: 3 bags appear on screen, each containing 4 apples. Bags clearly separated, total 12 apples visible.
Equation builder appears below the bags. Template shows three blank slots with multiplication and equals signs. Tile palette displays numbers 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14.

beats.text: You know how to do this. Build the equation shown by the picture.
Build the equation.

validator.description: Student placed 3 × 4 = 12, correct

params.description: Placed tiles lock into slots confirming 3 × 4 = 12.

beats.text: Right. 3 groups of 4. 3 times 4 equals 12. You've got equation building down.

params.description: The equals sign in the equation subtly highlights, drawing attention to it.

beats.text: But now, let's look more closely at that equals sign.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect equation on first attempt

beats.text: Check the bags. How many bags do you see? How many apples in each?

validator.description: Student placed incorrect equation on second attempt

params.description: Bags highlight to draw attention to the groups.

beats.text: Let's count together. I see 3 bags. In each bag, I count 4 apples. The first slot needs the number of bags. The second slot needs how many are in each bag. The last slot is the total.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect equation three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates placing tiles: first 3, then 4, then 12. Each tile slides into position.

beats.text: Here's how this works. First, I count the bags: 3. That goes in the first slot. Then I count what's in each bag: 4. That goes in the second slot. Finally, I count all the apples: 12. That's the product, so it goes in the last slot. The equation is 3 times 4 equals 12. 3 groups of 4 equals 12 total.

elements.description: 3 bags each containing 4 apples, total 12 apples visible.
Equation builder showing 3 × 4 = 12 with equals sign highlighted. Tile palette visible.

params.description: Static equation appears: 3 × 4 = 12.

beats.text: See this sign right here? The equals sign. Here's something important about it.

elements.description: Static equation: 3 × 4 = 12.

params.description: Equals sign glows and pulses.

beats.text: It means SAME VALUE AS. 3 times 4 has the same value as 12. Not the answer is. Equals means same value.

elements.description: Equation: 3 × 4 = 12. Equals sign highlighted.

beats.text: And since both sides have the same value, we can flip them around.

params.description: Equation rearranges to 12 = 3 × 4. Original fades, reversed stays.

beats.text: 12 equals 3 times 4. Same equation. Same value on both sides. Just written differently.

elements.description: Equation now shows: 12 = 3 × 4.

params.description: Reversed equation appears: 12 = 3 × 4.
Original equation reappears: 3 × 4 = 12. Both equations now visible side by side.

beats.text: Both of these are correct. The equals sign works both ways, because it means same value as.

elements.description: Equation: 3 × 4 = 12.
Equation: 12 = 3 × 4. Both equations visible side by side.

params.description: Equal groups visual appears: 6 boxes, 7 crayons in each.
Equation builder appears with template: blank × blank = blank. Tile palette: 5, 6, 7, 8, 13, 36, 40, 42, 48, 49.

beats.text: Your turn. Build the equation for these boxes.
Build the equation for these boxes.

validator.description: Student placed 6 × 7 = 42, correct

beats.text: You got it. 6 times 7 equals 42. Six boxes, 7 in each, same value as 42.

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers on first attempt

beats.text: Check the picture. How many boxes? How many crayons in each box?

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers on second attempt

params.description: Boxes highlight one at a time: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.

beats.text: Let's count together. Count the boxes first: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. That's your first number. Now count what's in one box. How many crayons?

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Boxes highlight one at a time as guide counts: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.
Items in first box highlight as guide counts: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7.

beats.text: Let me show you. First I count the boxes: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. That's the first number, 6 groups. Then I count what's in one box: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. That's 7 in each group. So I place 6, then 7, then the product. 6 times 7. Skip count by 7s six times: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35, 42. The answer is 6 times 7 equals 42. Six boxes, 7 crayons in each, 42 total.

elements.description: Equal groups visual: 6 boxes, 7 crayons in each.
Equation builder with template: blank × blank = blank. Tile palette active.

params.description: Reversed equation appears below: 42 = 6 × 7.

beats.text: And we can also write it this way. Same value, both sides.

elements.description: Equal groups visual: 6 boxes, 7 crayons in each.
Equation builder showing placed equation: 6 × 7 = 42.
Reversed equation: 42 = 6 × 7.

params.description: Equal groups visual appears: 5 bags, 9 stars in each.
Equation builder appears with template: blank × blank = blank. Tile palette: 3, 5, 9, 14, 36, 40, 45, 50, 54, 63.

beats.text: Same idea. Count the bags, count what's inside, figure out the product. Build the equation.
Build the equation: blank × blank = blank

validator.description: Student placed 5 × 9 = 45, correct

beats.text: Right. 5 times 9 equals 45. Five bags, 9 in each, same value as 45.

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers on first attempt

beats.text: Look closely. Count the bags. Count what's inside each bag.

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers on second attempt

params.description: Bags highlight one at a time: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

beats.text: Here's what helps. Count the bags first: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. That gives you the groups. Now look inside one bag and count the stars. What do you get?

validator.description: Student placed wrong numbers three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Bags highlight one at a time as guide counts: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.
Stars in first bag highlight as guide counts: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: This one takes a bit of thought, so let's work through it together. I start by counting the bags: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. That's 5 groups. Then I pick one bag and count the stars inside: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. That's 9 in each group. So the equation is 5 times 9. Now I need the product. I can skip count by 9s five times: 9, 18, 27, 36, 45. The answer is 5 times 9 equals 45. Five bags, 9 stars in each, 45 total. That's how the equation matches the picture.

elements.description: Equal groups visual: 5 bags, 9 stars in each.
Equation builder with template: blank × blank = blank. Tile palette active.

params.description: Reversed equation appears: 45 = 5 × 9.

beats.text: Both ways work.

elements.description: Equal groups visual: 5 bags, 9 stars in each.
Equation builder showing placed equation: 5 × 9 = 45.
Reversed equation: 45 = 5 × 9.

params.description: Equal groups appear. 3 boxes, 8 cookies in each box.
Equation builder appears with reversed template: product slot = groups slot × items slot. Tile palette shows 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 11, 16, 20, 21, 24.

beats.text: Here's something different. The template is flipped. Product comes first this time. Remember, equals means same value as, so the product can go on either side. Build this equation with the product first.
Build the equation: __ = __ × __

validator.description: Student placed 24 = 3 × 8, correct

beats.text: Right. 24 equals 3 times 8. Product on the left, multiplication expression on the right. Same value on both sides.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect values on first attempt

beats.text: Check the picture. Count the boxes, count what's in each box.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect values on second attempt

params.description: The 3 boxes highlight.

beats.text: Three boxes. Count the cookies in one box: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8. That's 8 cookies per box. Now figure out the product — 3 groups of 8. Place those numbers into the equation.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect values three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Boxes highlight one at a time: 1, 2, 3.
Cookies in first box highlight one at a time: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8.
Tiles place into equation one at a time: 24 in product slot, 3 in groups slot, 8 in items slot.

beats.text: Here's how this works. I count the boxes: 1, 2, 3. Three groups. I count the cookies in one box: 8 cookies. So I have 3 groups of 8. Three times 8 equals 24. That's the product. The equation is 24 equals 3 times 8. Product first, expression second. Same value on both sides.

elements.description: Equal groups showing 3 boxes with 8 cookies in each box.
Equation builder with reversed template: __ = __ × __. Tile palette active with place_tile tool.

params.description: Equal groups appear. 4 bags, 6 marbles in each bag.
Equation builder appears below. Template: blank × blank = blank. Tile palette: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 10, 16, 18, 24, 28.

beats.text: One more with bags. Build the equation.
Build the equation that matches the picture.

validator.description: Student placed 4 × 6 = 24, correct

beats.text: Right. 4 times 6 equals 24. Four bags, 6 in each, same value as 24.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Count the bags, then count what's in each bag.

validator.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

params.description: Bags highlight one at a time in sequence: 1, 2, 3, 4.

beats.text: Let's count together. Four bags. Now count the marbles in one bag. How many do you see in each?

validator.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as guide counts: 1, 2, 3, 4.

beats.text: Let me show you. First, count the bags: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four bags, that's the first number. Now look inside one bag and count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Six marbles in each bag, that's the second number. Four groups of 6. Now multiply: 4 times 6 equals 24. The equation is 4 times 6 equals 24.

elements.description: Equal groups. 4 bags, 6 marbles in each bag.
Equation builder with template blank × blank = blank. Student placing tiles.

params.description: Equation builder updates to reversed template: 24 = 4 × 6.

beats.text: And we can write it this way too. Same value.

elements.description: Equal groups. 4 bags, 6 marbles in each bag.
Equation builder showing reversed template: 24 = 4 × 6.

params.description: Equal groups visual appears. 3 horizontal rows of books on shelves. 9 books per row. Books arranged in clear horizontal rows.
Equation builder appears with blank multiplication equation template. Tile palette shows numbers 3, 4, 8, 9, 10, 12, 24, 27, 30, 36.

beats.text: Equal groups don't just come in bags and boxes. They're everywhere. Rows of books. Stacks of plates. Groups of animals. The math works the same way.

elements.description: Equal groups visual showing 3 rows of books on shelves. 9 books per row.
Equation builder with blank multiplication template. Palette available.

beats.text: Look at these shelves. I see rows of books. Each row is a group, just like each bag was a group.

params.description: Rows highlight one at a time in sequence.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3. Three rows. That's 3 groups. And in each row?

elements.description: 3 rows of books. All rows highlighted after sequential animation.
Equation builder with blank template.

params.description: Items in first row highlight one at a time as guide counts.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. Nine books. So I've got 3 rows of 9. Same pattern: groups of items.

elements.description: 3 rows of books. First row's items highlighted.
Equation builder with blank template.

beats.text: 3 times 9. And the product? Skip count by 9s: 9, 18, 27.

params.description: Guide demonstration: tiles place into equation. 3 × 9 = 27 appears.

beats.text: 3 times 9 equals 27. Same equation building, different picture.

elements.description: 3 rows of books on shelves. First row highlighted.
Equation builder showing completed equation: 3 × 9 = 27.

params.description: Equation reverses to show commutative form: 27 = 3 × 9.

beats.text: Both ways, same value.

elements.description: 3 rows of books on shelves.
Equation builder showing reversed form: 27 = 3 × 9.

params.description: Four images displayed in a 2×2 grid, labeled A-D. A: 3 shelves with 6 jars on each shelf. B: 4 plates with different numbers of cookies: 5, 6, 5, 2. C: 2 equal stacks of 9 books. D: 3 groups of animals: 7 ducks, 4 ducks, 7 ducks. Each picture has 18 items total.

beats.text: Equal groups are everywhere, but not every group of things is an equal group. Look at these four pictures. Each picture has 18 items in total. Which ones show equal groups, the same number in every group?
Select ALL the pictures that show equal groups.

validator.description: Student selected A and C, both correct

beats.text: Right. The first picture has 3 shelves with 6 on each. Equal groups. The third picture has 2 stacks of 9. Equal groups. We can write multiplication equations for these. The other two? The groups aren't equal. Different amounts in different groups. We can't write a multiplication equation for those.

validator.description: Branch 2: Student selected only A or only C (correct options only, but incomplete)

params.description: Both A and C highlight to show they are the equal groups examples.

beats.text: You found one of them. Look at both A and C closely. They both show equal groups. Did you select both?

validator.description: Branch 3: Student selected only incorrect options (B and/or D), no correct answers

params.description: B and D highlight to draw attention to their unequal structure. A and C dim.

beats.text: Equal groups means the same number in every group. Look at B: 5, 6, 5, 2. Those aren't equal. Look at D: 7, 4, 7. Those aren't equal either. Find the pictures where every group has exactly the same count.

validator.description: Branch 4: Student selected a mix of correct (A or C) and incorrect (B or D)

params.description: A and C pulse green (equal groups). B and D pulse red (unequal groups).

beats.text: You found at least one equal group picture. But check the others you selected. Count the items in each group. Are they all the same number?

validator.description: Heavy: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights A: arrows point to each shelf showing 6, 6, 6. Then highlights C: arrows point to each stack showing 9, 9. Then shows B: 5, 6, 5, 2 with X marks. Then D: 7, 4, 7 with X marks.

beats.text: Let me show you. Picture A has 3 shelves: 6 jars, 6 jars, 6 jars. Equal groups. Picture C has 2 stacks: 9 books, 9 books. Equal groups. Picture B has 4 plates: 5, 6, 5, 2. Not equal. Picture D has 3 groups: 7 ducks, 4 ducks, 7 ducks. Not equal. A and C are the answers because every group in those pictures has the same count. That is what equal groups means.

elements.description: Four images displayed in a 2×2 grid, labeled A-D. A: 3 shelves with 6 jars on each shelf. B: 4 plates with different numbers of cookies: 5, 6, 5, 2. C: 2 equal stacks of 9 books. D: 3 groups of animals: 7 ducks, 4 ducks, 7 ducks.

params.description: 8 stacks of 4 plates appear. Plates clearly stacked.
Equation Builder appears with pre-filled equation: 8 × 4 = ☐. The ☐ is visible in the product slot. Palette shows answer tiles: 28, 30, 32, 36.

beats.text: 8 stacks of 4 plates. These are equal groups so we can write a multiplication equation to represent the picture. The expression part is done. 8 times 4. But see that box where the product goes? That's the unknown. The number we need to figure out. Find the total and place it.
8 × 4 = ☐. Find the product. Place the number.

validator.description: Student placed 32 in the product slot

params.description: 32 tile locks into the product slot, completing the equation.

beats.text: Right. 8 times 4 equals 32. You found the unknown. 32 is the product. 8 stacks, 4 in each, same value as 32.

validator.description: Student placed wrong number on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Count the plates in all 8 stacks.

validator.description: Student placed wrong number on second attempt

params.description: All 8 stacks highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Let's think about this together. Skip count by 4s, once for each stack. 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32. How many times did we count? Eight times. What's the total?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System counts plates sequentially: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32. Running total appears under each stack.

beats.text: Let me show you how this works. We skip count by 4s, once for each stack: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32. Eight stacks of 4 plates equals 32 total. That's what 8 times 4 means. The product is 32.

elements.description: 8 stacks of 4 plates. Equal groups representation with plates clearly stacked.
Equation Builder showing completed equation: 8 × 4 = 32. Product slot filled.

params.description: Equal groups visual appears. 5 clusters of 7 sheep in a field. Sheep grouped by natural spatial proximity with clear gaps between clusters. No circles or containers.
Equation builder appears with reversed template. Pre-filled: ☐ = 5 × 7. Palette shows tiles: 12, 30, 35, 42.

beats.text: Look at this field. See the sheep? They're grouped together equally. 5 groups, 7 in each. The equation is flipped. Unknown first this time. But equals still means same value as. Figure out the product and place it.
☐ = 5 × 7. What is the product?

validator.description: Student placed 35, correct product

params.description: 35 tile slides into the unknown slot, equation completes: 35 = 5 × 7.

beats.text: Right. 35 equals 5 times 7. The unknown was 35. Same value on both sides.

validator.description: Student placed wrong product tile on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Count the sheep in one cluster, then skip count by that number for all 5 clusters.

validator.description: Student placed wrong product tile on second attempt

params.description: First cluster of 7 sheep highlights.

beats.text: Here's what helps: one cluster has 7 sheep. Now skip count by 7s for the 5 clusters: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35. What's the product?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Clusters highlight one at a time as guide skip counts: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35.

beats.text: Let me show you. One cluster has 7 sheep. I skip count by 7s: first cluster, 7. Second cluster, 14. Third, 21. Fourth, 28. Fifth, 35. Five groups of 7 equals 35. The product is 35 because 5 times 7 has the same value as 35.

elements.description: Equal groups visual showing 5 clusters of 7 sheep in a field. Natural spatial clustering with clear gaps.
Equation builder with reversed template. Completed equation: 35 = 5 × 7.

params.description: Equal groups visual appears: 2 shelves with 8 jars each.

beats.text: New context, same pattern of equal groups. 2 shelves of 8 jars. Find the product.
2 × 8 = ☐. Find the product.

validator.description: Student placed 16 in the product slot, correct

beats.text: Right. 2 times 8 equals 16. Two shelves, 8 in each, 16 total.

params.description: Equation builder updates to show reversed version: 16 = 2 × 8.

beats.text: Same value, both ways.

validator.description: Student placed a wrong tile on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Count all the jars. How many total?

validator.description: Student placed a wrong tile on second attempt

params.description: Both shelves highlight.

beats.text: Let's count together. First shelf has 8 jars. Second shelf has 8 jars. 8 plus 8. What's the total?

validator.description: Student placed a wrong tile three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 16 tile slides into the product slot, completing 2 × 8 = 16.

beats.text: Let me show you. First shelf, 8 jars. Second shelf, 8 jars. 8 plus 8 equals 16. Or we can skip count by 8s: 8, 16. Either way, 2 times 8 equals 16. That's the product.

elements.description: Equal groups visual showing 2 shelves with 8 jars each.
Equation builder showing 2 × 8 = ☐ with palette tiles 10, 14, 16, 18. Place_tile tool active.

params.description: 4 clusters of 5 birds each, all clusters faded. Total shown: 20 birds total.
Equation Builder with pre-filled template: blank × 5 = 20. Language prompt above: blank groups of 5 equals 20 total.

beats.text: Here's the new unknown. I know there are 5 birds in each group. And I know the total is 20 birds. But I don't know how many groups. That's the unknown.

elements.description: 4 clusters of 5 birds each, all clusters faded. Total label: 20 birds total.
Equation Builder: blank × 5 = 20. Language prompt: blank groups of 5 equals 20 total.

beats.text: Let me read the sentence: HOW MANY groups of 5 equals 20 total? I need to figure out how many groups of 5 make 20.

elements.description: 4 clusters of 5 birds each, all clusters faded. Total label: 20 birds total.
Equation Builder: blank × 5 = 20. Language prompt: blank groups of 5 equals 20 total.

beats.text: I can skip-count by 5s. I know how to do that. Watch: 5...

beats.text: I counted 4 times. That means 4 groups. 4 groups of 5 equals 20.

All 4 clusters fully visible, no longer highlighted.

elements.description: All 4 clusters of 5 birds fully visible. Total label: 20 birds total.
Equation Builder: 4 × 5 = 20.

beats.text: The unknown was 4. I used skip-counting, something I already know, to figure out how many groups.

Equation Builder: 4 × 5 = 20.

params.description: Equal Groups with Pictures in Construction Mode appears. Construction area empty. Running total display: Total: 0 / 40.
Equation Builder appears with pre-filled template: ☐ × 10 = 40. Language prompt: __ groups of 10 equals 40 total.

beats.text: Now you try. Groups of 10. Total is 40. How many groups do you need? Build groups until you hit 40.
☐ × 10 = 40. Build groups of 10 until the total reaches 40.

validator.description: Student built 4 groups of 10, total = 40

params.description: 4 groups of 10 highlight to confirm total = 40.

validator.description: Student built 3 groups of 10, total = 30, short of target

beats.text: That's 3 groups of 10, which is 30. You need 40 total. Add one more group.

validator.description: Student built 6 groups of 10, total = 60, overshot

beats.text: That's 60, too many. Remove groups until you reach 40.

validator.description: Student built wrong number of groups on first attempt

beats.text: Count by 10s as you add groups. Stop when you hit 40.

validator.description: Student built wrong number of groups on second attempt

params.description: Running total display emphasized: Total: 0 / 40. Target of 40 highlighted.

beats.text: You need exactly 40. Count by 10s: one group is 10, two groups is 20, three groups is 30. How many groups to reach 40?

validator.description: Student built wrong number of groups three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Guide demonstrates building groups: one group (10), two groups (20), three groups (30), four groups (40). Each group appears sequentially with running count.

beats.text: Let me show you. One group of 10 is 10. Two groups is 20. Three groups is 30. Four groups is 40. It takes 4 groups of 10 to make 40. The unknown was 4.

elements.description: Equal Groups with Pictures in Construction Mode. Student building groups of 10. Running total updates live. set_container_count tool active.
Equation Builder with template: ☐ × 10 = 40.

params.description: 4 groups of 10 cats visible. Total = 40.

beats.text: 4 groups of 10 equals 40. You skip counted, 10, 20, 30, 40, and it took 4 groups.

params.description: Reversed form shown: 40 = 4 × 10.

elements.description: 4 groups of 10 cats visible. Total = 40.
Equation: 4 × 10 = 40. Reversed form shown: 40 = 4 × 10.

params.description: Equal Groups with Pictures in Construction Mode appears. 2 empty groups displayed, group count locked at 2. Items inside covered. Running total: Total: 0 / 10.
Equation Builder with pre-filled template: 2 × ☐ = 10. Language prompt: 2 groups of __ equals 10 total.

beats.text: Here's the equation. 2 groups of how many cats equals 10. We need to build the groups. Let's add equal items to the 2 groups until you reach 10. Add 5 to each group so the total reaches 10.
2 × ☐ = 10. Add 5 cats to each of the 2 groups to make 10 total.

validator.description: Student set 5 items per container, 2 equal groups of 5, total = 10

params.description: 2 groups of 5 cats highlight to confirm total = 10.

validator.description: Student put 4 in each group, total = 8, short of target

beats.text: That's 8 total. You need 10. Add one more to each group.

validator.description: Student put 6 in each group, total = 12, overshot

beats.text: That's 12, too many. Remove one from each group.

validator.description: Student set wrong number per group on first attempt

beats.text: Check the total. You need 10. Adjust the number in each group.

validator.description: Student set wrong number per group on second attempt

params.description: Running total display emphasized: Total shows current count / 10. Target of 10 highlighted.

beats.text: You have 2 groups. Each group needs the same number. Count by that number twice to reach 10. Try different amounts and watch the total.

validator.description: Student set wrong number per group three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Guide demonstrates building: 5 cats appear in first group, then 5 in second group. Running count shows: 5, then 10.

beats.text: Let me show you. If I put 5 in the first group, that's 5. Then 5 in the second group brings the total to 10. Two groups of 5 equals 10. The unknown was 5.

elements.description: 2 groups of 5 cats visible. Total = 10.
Equation: 2 × 5 = 10.

2 × ☐ = 14. How many in each group?

beats.text: You built equations with bags, rows, stacks, and groups. You found unknown numbers in different positions. Let's see what you know.
